surface.name: Deploy and verify the first AWS S3 bucket with Pulumi TypeScript
surface.selection_basis: Workshop selection policy (2026-07-19): when official docs present peer routes, commit to the fastest and most commonly used documented route. Prefer the vendor's first-listed or recommended quickstart, dashboard/console over CLI when both are peers, and the route with an explicit vendor time claim or fewest documented steps when tied. Selected route: Deploy and verify the first AWS S3 bucket with Pulumi TypeScript. The product and provider router has no default, but the AWS IaC tutorial documents a first deploy that creates an empty S3 bucket and verifies it through the exported `bucketName` before the later website update. Prior surface before policy commit: Pulumi product, infrastructure provider, and language not selected.

alternatives_considered.name: AWS IaC first S3 bucket deployment
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is one cloud-provider tutorial and still requires TypeScript, Python, Go, C#, Java, or YAML selection. Its first deploy creates an empty S3 bucket; the website is a later update.

alternatives_considered.name: Azure IaC
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Azure uses different account, CLI, authentication, resource types, and verification.

alternatives_considered.name: Google Cloud IaC
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Google Cloud has separate credentials, APIs, and deployment resources.

alternatives_considered.name: Kubernetes IaC
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This route requires an existing cluster and kubectl access rather than creating cloud-provider infrastructure.

alternatives_considered.name: Pulumi ESC
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Secrets and Configuration is a separate Pulumi product with a first environment rather than infrastructure deployment.

entry_point.developer_intent: Reach a first Pulumi result without a preselected Pulumi product, infrastructure provider, or programming language.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has arrived after discovery.
No Pulumi product, provider, credentials, state backend, stack, language, or infrastructure target is selected.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: No Pulumi platform-wide first-success milestone is selected by the Get Started router.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: AWS CLI reaches the new bucket and returns an empty listing without error.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: AWS CLI reaches the new bucket and returns an empty listing without error.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: AWS CLI reaches the new bucket and returns an empty listing without error.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: Selected under workshop fastest/most-used policy; terminal taken from the committed official candidate route.

prerequisites.requirement: Select Pulumi IaC, Secrets and Configuration, Insights and Governance, or a solution tutorial.
A selected IaC cloud branch requires the target cloud account and credentials; Kubernetes requires an accessible cluster.
IaC requires Pulumi CLI plus the runtime and package manager for TypeScript, Python, Go, C#, Java, or YAML as documented by the selected tutorial.

primary_path.action: Use Pulumi Get Started to choose the Pulumi product and intended infrastructure job.
details: The router lists IaC, ESC, Insights and Governance, Kubernetes clusters, websites, and serverless applications.
IaC branches by AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, and Kubernetes.
primary_path.success_signal: No shared terminal state exists at this router.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot be atomic before these choices.

primary_path.action: For IaC, choose provider and supported language, then enter that tutorial's install, access, project, and deploy sequence.
details: AWS's first deploy creates an empty S3 bucket and supports TypeScript, Python, Go, C#, Java, and YAML. Turning it into a website is the next Make an update page.
The common sequence includes CLI installation, provider access, `pulumi new`, stack configuration, preview, `pulumi up`, and verification.
primary_path.input: Human-selected provider, account, credentials, language, and state backend.
primary_path.output: A bounded provider and language tutorial.
primary_path.success_signal: The selected tutorial can terminate at deployed infrastructure.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Authentication, billing, resource provisioning, prompt text, outputs, and verification differ by branch.

primary_path.action: Create or obtain access to the AWS account that will own the tutorial resources.
details: The selected identity must be allowed to create the generated S3 bucket and AWS use can incur provider charges.
primary_path.input: AWS identity, account, and required permissions.
primary_path.success_signal: The developer can obtain working AWS credentials.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing account, billing, policy, or S3 permission blocks the deployment.

primary_path.action: Install the TypeScript runtime prerequisites, Pulumi CLI, and AWS CLI used by the verification command.
details: Other supported language routes use Python, Go, C#, Java, or YAML instead.
primary_path.output: Node.js package tools, Pulumi CLI, and AWS CLI.
primary_path.success_signal: The selected runtime, `pulumi`, and `aws` commands run locally.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A missing or unsupported tool stops project creation or verification.

primary_path.action: Authenticate Pulumi CLI to Pulumi Cloud or another supported state backend.
details: The backend stores the project stack state used by `pulumi up`.
primary_path.input: Pulumi account or selected backend credentials.
primary_path.success_signal: Pulumi can create and address a stack.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Backend account, token, URL, or network failure prevents stack creation.

primary_path.action: Configure AWS credentials for the target account and verify that the local AWS access works.
details: Pulumi uses the configured AWS identity when creating resources.
primary_path.input: AWS access credentials and region context.
primary_path.success_signal: The AWS access check succeeds for the intended account.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Expired, wrong-account, denied, or incomplete AWS credentials must be corrected before deployment.

primary_path.action: Create the AWS TypeScript Pulumi project and stack with the tutorial's `pulumi new` flow and answer its prompts.
details: The generated starter program declares one S3 bucket and exports its name.
primary_path.input: Authenticated Pulumi backend, AWS credentials, project name, stack name, and region prompt values.
primary_path.output: Generated Pulumi project and configured stack.
primary_path.success_signal: The project files and stack configuration are present.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Template, backend, package installation, prompt, name, or region errors prevent project creation.

primary_path.action: Inspect the generated TypeScript program and confirm that it declares the S3 bucket and `bucketName` stack output.
details: Website configuration and `index.html` are intentionally deferred to the tutorial's later update page.
primary_path.success_signal: The program contains the bucket resource and output used by verification.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A different or modified template changes the documented first resource and should be reconciled before continuing.

primary_path.action: Run `pulumi up`, inspect the preview, and select `yes` to approve the update.
details: The preview shows the S3 bucket resource that will be created.
primary_path.input: Configured project, stack, backend, and AWS credentials.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Preview errors, unexpected resources, denied approval, configuration, credential, or policy failures stop the update.

primary_path.action: Wait for Pulumi to create the AWS resource and finish the stack update.
details: The update summary reports the bucket created and prints the `bucketName` output.
primary_path.output: Created AWS S3 bucket and stack output.
primary_path.success_signal: Pulumi reports the S3 bucket created and emits `bucketName`.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: AWS provisioning, permission, name, quota, network, or state errors require correction and another update.

primary_path.action: Run `aws s3 ls s3://$(pulumi stack output bucketName)`.
details: The first bucket is intentionally empty.
primary_path.input: Completed stack, AWS CLI, and exported bucket name.
primary_path.success_signal: AWS CLI reaches the new bucket and returns an empty listing without error.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A missing output, wrong AWS identity, permission error, or unavailable bucket means the documented verification has not succeeded.

branches.path: Choose a language and install its runtime, obtain an AWS account, install Pulumi CLI, authenticate Pulumi to Pulumi Cloud or another supported backend, configure AWS credentials and verify access, create the AWS project and stack, review the generated S3 bucket code, run `pulumi up`, inspect the preview, select yes, and wait for the S3 bucket. Confirm the update reports the bucket created and outputs `bucketName`, then run `aws s3 ls s3://$(pulumi stack output bucketName)` and confirm the new bucket is reachable and empty.
branches.effect_on_first_success: An empty AWS S3 bucket is deployed and verified. Adding website configuration, ownership controls, public access, `index.html`, a URL output, a second `pulumi up`, and `curl` belongs to the documented Make an update page after this boundary.

branches.condition: Azure or Google Cloud IaC is selected.
branches.path: Follow the corresponding provider's Install Pulumi, Configure access, Create project, and Deploy stack pages with that cloud's authentication and resource example.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The created resources and verification signal are provider-specific.

branches.path: Obtain a local or cloud-managed Kubernetes cluster, install and configure kubectl, verify `kubectl cluster-info` or `kubectl get nodes`, then follow the Kubernetes project and deployment tutorial.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Pulumi manages resources inside an existing cluster rather than provisioning the same AWS website.

friction_gates.description: Pulumi product, provider, workload, and language are explicit preconditions to one first-mile journey.
Pulumi CLI and language runtime or package manager must be installed.
Pulumi backend authentication and provider credentials are required before deployment.
Cloud-provider resources can create billable infrastructure according to the selected provider account.
`pulumi up` waits for provider resource provisioning after the developer approves the preview.
Kubernetes route requires an existing accessible cluster and configured kubectl context.

sources.title: Get started with Pulumi and AWS
evidence_supported: AWS first-bucket route and later website-update distinction

sources.title: Deploy the Stack with Pulumi and AWS
evidence_supported: First pulumi up preview and approval
Website deferred to Make an update

uncertainties.question: Which Pulumi product, provider, workload, state backend, and language should define first success?
uncertainties.impact: The choice changes accounts, billing, credentials, software, source code, created resources, wait time, and verification.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Pulumi's official Get Started page is explicitly a chooser across products and providers.

excluded_after_success: No common after-success scope is selected while provider and language remain unresolved.
Updating infrastructure, reusable components, destroying resources, policy, CI/CD, migrations, production hardening, and Pulumi Deployments are excluded.
